controls.title: Understanding the organization and its context
controls.description: Determine external and internal issues relevant to AI management system.

controls.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
controls.description: Determine interested parties and their requirements relevant to the AIMS.

controls.title: Determining the scope of the AIMS
controls.description: Determine boundaries and applicability of the AI management system.

controls.title: AI management system
controls.description: Establish, implement, maintain and continually improve the AIMS.

controls.title: Leadership and commitment
controls.description: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment.

controls.title: AI policy
controls.description: Top management shall establish an AI policy appropriate to the organization.

controls.title: Roles, responsibilities and authorities
controls.description: Top management shall ensure responsibilities and authorities are assigned and communicated.

controls.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities
controls.description: Plan actions to address risks and opportunities for the AIMS.

controls.title: AI risk assessment
controls.description: Define and apply an AI risk assessment process.

controls.title: AI risk treatment
controls.description: Define and apply an AI risk treatment process.

controls.title: AI system impact assessment
controls.description: Conduct impact assessments for AI systems considering individuals, groups, and societies.

controls.title: AI objectives and planning to achieve them
controls.description: Establish AI objectives at relevant functions, levels, and processes.

controls.title: Resources
controls.description: Determine and provide resources needed for the AIMS.

controls.title: Competence
controls.description: Determine competence of persons doing work that affects AI performance.

controls.title: Awareness
controls.description: Persons doing work shall be aware of the AI policy and their contribution.

controls.title: Documented information
controls.description: The AIMS shall include documented information required by the standard.

controls.title: Operational planning and control
controls.description: Plan, implement and control processes needed to meet requirements.

controls.title: AI risk assessment (operational)
controls.description: Perform AI risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

controls.title: AI risk treatment (operational)
controls.description: Implement the AI risk treatment plan.

controls.title: AI system impact assessment (operational)
controls.description: Perform AI system impact assessments at planned intervals.

controls.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
controls.description: Determine what needs to be monitored and measured.

controls.title: Internal audit
controls.description: Conduct internal audits at planned intervals.

controls.title: Management review
controls.description: Top management shall review the AIMS at planned intervals.

controls.title: Continual improvement
controls.description: Continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the AIMS.

controls.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
controls.description: React to nonconformities and take corrective action.

controls.title: Policies related to AI
controls.description: AI policies shall address responsible development and use.

controls.title: Internal organization
controls.description: AI roles and responsibilities shall be defined within the organization.

controls.title: Resources for AI systems
controls.description: Resources including data, tools, and infrastructure for AI systems.

controls.title: Assessing impacts of AI systems
controls.description: Impacts of AI systems on individuals, groups, and societies shall be assessed.

controls.title: AI system life cycle
controls.description: Controls for development, testing, deployment, operation, and decommissioning.

controls.title: Data for AI systems
controls.description: Data acquisition, quality, provenance, and governance for AI systems.

controls.title: Information for interested parties of AI systems
controls.description: Transparency and communication about AI systems to interested parties.

controls.title: Use of AI systems
controls.description: Appropriate use of AI systems and processes for responsible deployment.

controls.title: Third-party and customer relationships
controls.description: Managing AI-related risks from third parties and in customer relationships.

controls.title: Societal norms and values
controls.description: Consider societal norms and values in AI development and deployment.

controls.title: Human oversight of AI systems
controls.description: Human oversight mechanisms for AI system decisions and actions.

controls.title: Explainability and interpretability
controls.description: AI system decisions should be explainable and interpretable as appropriate.

controls.title: AI system security
controls.description: Goal-state verification and input validation as AIMS security design requirements � structural controls enforced at orchestration layer; Per-tool permission manifests and parameter validation as AIMS security controls; Credential security as AIMS design requirement � short-lived JIT credentials, no cleartext storage, least privilege

controls.title: Testing of AI systems
controls.description: Goal hijack scenarios in AIMS testing � direct, indirect, multi-turn injection tested before each production release; Sandbox escape and code injection scenarios in AIMS testing � adversarial testing before each deployment; A2A security scenarios in AIMS testing � spoofing, replay, schema violations before deployment

controls.title: Impact assessment
controls.description: Impact assessment covers goal hijack risk � what autonomous actions are possible if goal is redirected, who is affected; Tool misuse impact assessed � what harm is possible if each tool is misused autonomously; Impact of code execution capability formally assessed � RCE impact on persons and systems documented

controls.title: Risk assessment
controls.description: Goal hijack in AI risk register � blast radius per deployment, owner, treatment, review cadence; Code execution risk in AI risk register � blast radius, sandbox status, permitted operations documented; Cascade blast radius in AI risk register � maximum affected systems formally documented and accepted

controls.title: Responsible AI system management
controls.description: Tool access managed responsibly throughout agent lifecycle � permission review, irreversibility classification, human oversight requirements; Comprehensive audit logging and rogue agent containment as responsible lifecycle management obligation; LLM components managed responsibly through lifecycle � acquisition, testing, deployment, decommission

controls.title: Third-party AI system acquisition
controls.description: Tool and MCP server providers assessed as third-party AI components � security obligations in contracts; Third-party services accessed via agent credentials assessed � security obligations in access arrangements; All agent tool and MCP server providers assessed � security obligations, integrity guarantees, disclosure SLA in contracts

controls.title: Data provenance and characteristics
controls.description: Agent credentials tracked as AI system data � issuance, scope, expiry, rotation documented; Memory provenance tracked � source, write access controls, TTL, modification history in AIMS; Data provenance documented � source, classification, handling requirements tracked from ingestion through all derived forms

controls.title: Support
controls.description: Resources for AI systems include NHIs � agent identities inventoried and managed as AIMS resources; Documented information controls � system prompts managed as AIMS documented information with appropriate access controls

controls.title: Customer relationships
controls.description: Obligations to downstream consumers of agentic systems � what supply chain security is guaranteed; LLM deployment obligations to downstream customers � what security properties are guaranteed

controls.title: Data quality
controls.description: Training data from third-party sources assessed � same data quality criteria as internal data; Agent memory content quality requirements � access controls, integrity verification, TTL as data quality controls; Training data and RAG content quality requirements � sensitivity, completeness, appropriateness assessed before use

controls.title: Monitoring of AI systems
controls.description: Memory integrity monitored in operation � anomalous write patterns, content integrity checks as AIMS monitoring; Cascade indicators monitored in operation � correlated failure patterns detected as AIMS monitoring; Behavioural monitoring as AIMS operational control � baseline deviation detection is the primary rogue agent control

controls.title: Operation
controls.description: A2A communication documented as AIMS operational control � authentication requirements, encryption standards; Operational controls for LLM deployment � output handling requirements documented as AIMS operational procedures

controls.title: Performance evaluation
controls.description: Cascade incidents in AIMS performance evaluation � circuit breaker effectiveness, recovery times in management review; Rogue agent detection rates and containment times in AIMS management review; Resource consumption metrics in AIMS performance evaluation � consumption trends in management review

controls.title: Information for interested parties
controls.description: AI system transparency obligations � users informed of AI nature, advisory status, limitations, EU AI Act Art. 50 alignment; Transparency obligations balanced with operational security � what must be disclosed vs what may be kept confidential; Transparency about AI system limitations � users informed of advisory status, accuracy limitations, verification requirements

controls.title: Use of AI systems
controls.description: Guidance on appropriate use � domains requiring human verification, how to distinguish AI advisory from authoritative content; Guidance on AI system use � downstream consumers informed that LLM output must be validated before use; Guidance on appropriate AI system use � domains requiring human verification documented as AIMS use guidance

controls.title: Policy
controls.description: Leadership commitment to AI transparency � AI disclosure and advisory labelling requirements in AI policy; Leadership commitment to human oversight � autonomous action scope defined in AI policy, signed off at executive level
